prompt: Our release-notes generator started crashing the week the Osaka team's repos joined the release train, and now the whole changelog job is blocked on it. Symptoms: any repo with Japanese or emoji-bearing commit subjects aborts the entire run — the CI wrapper eats the panic text, so all I get is a nonzero exit and the correlation with which repos are in the batch. And separately, French subject lines that are clearly INSIDE the 72-character budget come out clipped with an … they never earned. Pure-ASCII repos have run clean for over a year. Nothing about the clipping contract changed: budgets are counted in characters; a value within its budget passes through untouched; anything longer keeps its first budget-minus-one characters plus a single … so a clipped value lands exactly on budget; a commit subject is the first line of the message with trailing whitespace dropped, budget 72. `cargo test --offline` reproduces both the crash and the French mis-clip. Fix src/lib.rs — tests/, Cargo.toml, and .gitignore stay exactly as they are.
